questions: Does the implementation meet all ticket requirements and continuation context acceptance criteria?
Are there correctness issues, regressions, or missed requirements in the artifact commands, workflow, or documentation?
Does the implementation follow existing codebase patterns and maintain zero runtime dependencies?

answers.answer: Yes. All 8 acceptance criteria from the continuation context are met: (1) README sufficient for new engineer, (2) artifact retrieval commands for ticket/run via Helix backend, (3) clear errors for missing credentials and no-result cases, (4) hlx --version matches package.json 1.2.0, (5) GitHub Actions workflow publishes on version change only, (6) workflow fails when build output is missing, (7) no direct GitHub/Vercel API dependency, (8) diff scoped to helix-cli only.
evidence: README.md contains install instructions, Node >=18, auth docs, command reference, artifact examples, maintainer publish setup
src/artifacts/ticket.ts calls GET /api/tickets/:ticketId/artifacts via hxFetch with basePath '/api'
src/artifacts/run.ts validates --step and --repo-key with clear error messages before calling the step-artifacts endpoint
.github/workflows/publish.yml has version-change detection with conditional npm publish
Workflow checks dist/index.js existence and fails if missing
No imports of GitHub/Vercel APIs — all artifact retrieval goes through hxFetch to Helix backend
All changes in helix-cli: package.json, src/index.ts, src/artifacts/*.ts, .github/workflows/publish.yml, README.md

answers.answer: No correctness issues or regressions found. One minor usability improvement applied: CLI usage text in src/index.ts and src/artifacts/index.ts was missing the [--run <run-id>] optional filter for the ticket command. The feature was implemented and documented in the README, but not shown in the CLI's own help output. Fixed for consistency with the comments module pattern.
evidence: Build (npm run build) passes with exit 0 after fix
Typecheck (npm run typecheck) passes with exit 0 after fix
All error handling verified: missing --step, missing --repo-key, unauthenticated access, no subcommand
npm pack --dry-run shows all artifact files in package: dist/artifacts/index.js, ticket.js, run.js
Usage text now shows [--run <run-id>] for artifacts ticket command

answers.answer: Yes. The artifacts module mirrors the comments module structure exactly: router (src/artifacts/index.ts) with resolveTicketId, getFlag, subcommand switch; subcommand files (ticket.ts, run.ts) using hxFetch with basePath '/api', typed responses, and human-readable console.log output. Zero runtime dependencies added. ESM-only. createRequire used for version reading (Node.js builtin).
evidence: src/artifacts/index.ts follows src/comments/index.ts pattern: resolveTicketId, getFlag, switch/case routing
src/artifacts/ticket.ts follows src/comments/list.ts pattern: hxFetch with basePath, typed response, empty state handling
All new code uses ES module imports consistent with type: module

statement: Code review complete for helix-cli. All 7 changed files reviewed against ticket requirements, continuation context, product spec, and implementation plan. One minor usability fix applied: added [--run <run-id>] to CLI usage text in src/index.ts and src/artifacts/index.ts to document the optional run filter that was implemented but not shown in help output. Build and typecheck pass after fix. No correctness issues, no regressions, no missed requirements found. Implementation is well-structured, follows existing module patterns, and meets all acceptance criteria.
